data.narrative:
Parry Labs is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $80M+ to date. Valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all at Series B stage in the same sector.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $80M total capital raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $152M. Peer-median analysis across six same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.2B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The peer set demonstrates meaningful dispersion, with Castelion and Defense Unicorns valued at $1.0B and Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $129M to $1.4B, with a median of $676M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployment history, while peer comparables reflect market pricing for functionally similar businesses. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, customer concentration in government contracting, and technology differentiation within the defense tech cohort. An additional round of diligence on growth trajectory and competitive positioning would narrow this range meaningfully.
